A.rationale: Tightest, fully grounded; all layers, isolation caught, atomic-write bonus risk. But buries sort-direction ambiguity in Q1 rather than naming it the top risk.

B.rationale: Most complete risk set; elevates sort-direction as #1 semantic risk, flags pytest-vs-unittest run caveat and bool-is-int. Longest and slightly padded across assumptions/risks/questions.

C.rationale: Sharp: sort-direction as #1 risk, hermetic sort-helper extraction step, flags missing linter/type-checker. Tests lean parser-level over full cmd_add drive.

notes: All three excellent and at plan altitude. B and C elevate sort-direction ambiguity as the top risk (A buries it). B edges out on the most complete risk/test coverage; C is tighter with a hermetic-test idea; A is crispest but under-weights the semantic risk.
